user_query:
I’m kicking off a multi-step initiative called "Q2 Onboarding Revamp" and I want all of the work tracked as structured task notes that are easy to query and resume later.

Please do the following, reading all inputs from input/ and writing everything you create to output/:

1) Read these inputs:
- input/brief.md (initiative goals and scope)
- input/tasks.csv (columns: slug,title,assignee,priority — one row per subtask)
- input/context.md (decisions already made, key paths, and references)

2) Create a parent task note for the initiative at:
- output/tasks/Q2-Onboarding-Revamp.md
Requirements for the parent task note:
- YAML frontmatter at the top with at least these fields: 
  - type: task
  - title: "Q2 Onboarding Revamp"
  - description: one-paragraph summary distilled from input/brief.md
  - status: active
  - priority: high
  - steps: an array with at least 3 concrete, checkable steps tailored to the initiative
  - current_step: 1 (1-indexed)
  - started: today’s date in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD)
- Body must include these sections in Markdown:
  - "## Observations" with bullet lines that expose key fields in bracket form (exactly these keys must appear at least once):
    - - [description] ...
    - - [status] ...
    - - [assigned_to] (use your name or leave blank if not applicable)
    - - [current_step] ...
  - "## Steps" with numbered checkboxes (e.g., "1. [ ] …") corresponding to the steps in frontmatter
  - "## Context" that concisely summarizes input/context.md for future resumption and explicitly mentions the files input/brief.md and input/context.md as sources

3) For each row in input/tasks.csv, create a child task note at:
- output/tasks/<slug>.md  (use the slug column as the filename, keeping the .md extension)
Requirements for each child task note:
- YAML frontmatter with at least these fields:
  - type: task
  - title: from the CSV title column
  - description: a one-sentence summary aligned to input/brief.md
  - status: active
  - assigned_to: from the CSV assignee column
  - priority: from the CSV priority column
  - parent_task: "Q2 Onboarding Revamp"
  - steps: an array with at least 3 concrete, checkable steps based on the CSV title and the brief
  - current_step: 1 (1-indexed)
  - started: today’s date in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD)
- Body must include:
  - "## Observations" containing at minimum these bullet lines with bracketed keys:
    - - [description] ...
    - - [status] ...
    - - [assigned_to] ...
    - - [current_step] ...
  - "## Steps" with numbered checkboxes ("1. [ ] …") matching the frontmatter steps
  - "## Context" that summarizes the most relevant points from input/context.md for this specific subtask and mentions input/brief.md or input/context.md by path

4) Create a machine-readable index at:
- output/tasks/index.json
Structure:
{
  "initiative": "Q2 Onboarding Revamp",
  "tasks": [
    {"slug": "<slug>", "title": "<title>", "assigned_to": "<assignee>", "status": "active", "priority": "<priority>"},
    ... one object per CSV row ...
  ]
}

Notes:
- Keep all paths relative as shown (input/, output/...).
- Ensure current_step is 1 and that each steps array has length >= 3.
- Use lowercase "task" for the frontmatter type value.
- Make the steps concrete and checkable (e.g., "Inventory files in docs/", not "figure out docs").
- In each Context section, list at least the two input file paths you used and any relevant workspace paths you infer from input/context.md.

Deliverables:
- output/tasks/Q2-Onboarding-Revamp.md
- output/tasks/<slug>.md for each row in input/tasks.csv
- output/tasks/index.json
